mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 0.2pp monthly increase threshold
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the specified threshold during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO uptrends coincide with negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations during early-cycle expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary environment where real discount rates remain elevated, forcing equity deleveraging and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of instances when both CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% and FEDFUNDS remains above 3.5% during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls provides a stable baseline for consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing consistently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth series shows zero or negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate falling below 1.5 percent signals severe demand contraction, prompting immediate market repricing of recession risks and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 crosses below 1.5% quarterly growth threshold
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below the 1.5 percent threshold during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and stable unemployment indicates productivity-driven growth rather than labor hoarding, which expands profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence signal produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of triggered instances or the interaction coefficient flips sign in walk-forward validation.
